destinations.evidence: Scheduled filtering and full verification reject any signature other than r=0, s=0, v=false before state-heavy checks.
Alloy exposes scheduled signatures through the envelope signature accessor and as_scheduled helper.
Port-generated scheduled system transactions are signed with the dummy zero signature.
destinations.snippet:
let signature = Signature::new(U256::ZERO, U256::ZERO, false);
let signed = tx.into_signed(signature);

equivalence_argument: Mapped across port and alloy: generated scheduled transactions use the dummy signature, verifiers reject non-dummy signatures, and raw signatures are exposed for that check.
rationale: The current full verifier checks signature fields before state reads and the SignedTransaction trait exposes signature() for scheduled and other transaction envelopes.
statement: Full scheduled transaction verification and scheduled block-building filtering must reject scheduled transactions unless their signature is exactly r=0, s=0, v=false, while SignedTransaction implementations expose raw signatures needed for that check.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct scheduled tx infos with nonzero r, nonzero s, and v=true; assert filtering and full verification reject each.
summary: Scheduled verification enforces dummy system signatures.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Existing C7 tests cover nonzero r/s and wrong v; keep them as regression coverage.
title: Scheduled transaction verification requires dummy system signatures
